title: hieracium calcareum subsp. illyricum
intro: Hieracium calcareum subsp. illyricum, also known as Illyrian hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, digestive issues, and other health conditions. The medicinal properties of Hieracium calcareum subsp. illyricum are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
